notes: Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Claude Code harness, XHigh reasoning effort: Pass rate 25.7% / Score 48.7% (rank 12 overall). Score component used per corpus convention. Mirrored from agents-last-exam.org/leaderboard. [score corrected during adversarial verify: proposed 48.7 -> 48.5, per verifier: Verified by fetching the raw source HTML (curl, not the summarizing WebFetch tool, which gave inconsistent numbers across repeated calls on the same table). The source page (snorkel.ai/leaderboard/agents-last-exam) embeds several DISTINCT leaderboard splits in its JSON payload: "overall" (label "Overall", 50 entries) and "ale_cli" (label "ALE-CLI", description "linux task only", 55 entries) are separate tables, not the same thing. For harness=Claude Code, model=claude-fable-5, effort=XHigh, there are two different rows, one per split: - "Overall" split: rank 12, pass_rate 25.7%, score 48.7% - "ALE-CLI" split: rank 13, pass_rate 23.8%, score 48.5% The claim's notes explicitly say "Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard)" and give pass rate 25.7% / score 48.7% / rank 12 — but those three numbers are actually the "Overall" split's row, not the "ALE-CLI" split's row. This is exactly the wrong-benchmark-variant pattern flagged in the task instructions (and matches this project's known variant-conflation failure mode from the prior dataset audit): the notes cite "ALE-CLI" by name, but the number pulled belongs to the sibling "Overall" split. The true ALE-CLI-split number for this exact model/harness/effort combination is pass rate 23.8%, score 48.5%, rank 13. Model, harness, and effort level are otherwise correctly identified (Claude Code harness, XHigh effort, claude-fable-5 is real and distinct from claude-opus-4-8 which has a similar 25.7% pass rate on the Overall split — likely the actual source of the mixup, since Opus 4-8's Overall pass rate of 25.7% appears to have been attached to Fable 5's ALE-CLI-labeled entry). Recommend correcting the score to 48.5 (and pass rate/rank to 23.8%/13 if those fields are tracked) if the corpus intends the ALE-CLI-specific split, or alternatively relabeling the notes to say "Overall leaderboard" (dropping the ALE-CLI qualifier) if 48.7 is the number actually wanted — but as currently worded, the notes and the number don't match the same split.]

Agents' Last Exam (ALE), Table 1 of the official Seed2.1 Model Card PDF. ByteDance reports Seed2.1-Pro as Pass@1/Score = 19.5/41.4; I use the Score component (41.4) as the headline figure, since it is independently corroborated by llm-stats.com's public Agents' Last Exam leaderboard, which lists Seed 2.1 Pro's score as 41.4% (0.414) with no other models yet tracked. Confirmed in the model card's prose ('On Agents' Last Exam, Seed2.1-Pro reaches 19.5 / 41.4, outperforming Claude-4.7-Opus and Gemini-3.1-Pro while approaching GPT-5.5').
